varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris
varieties.origin: Southeast Europe, Mediterranean region. Cultivated since ancient times.
varieties.traits: Upright bushy growth to 20-30cm tall, spreading 30-40cm. Small, narrow gray-green leaves, pale lilac to white flowers in late spring/early summer.
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity. Hardy perennial with continuous leaf production.
varieties.availability: Widely available at major humid subtropical climates nurseries: Ross Evans Garden Centre, Timbara Garden Centre, Pohlmans Nursery, Bunnings, local herb specialists.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris (French cultivar)
varieties.origin: Provence region, France. Selected cultivar.
varieties.traits: Narrower, gray-green leaves than English variety. Compact upright growth 15-25cm. Reddish-brown stems. Slightly sweeter appearance.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good. Slightly slower growing than English thyme.
varieties.availability: Available at specialty herb nurseries in humid subtropical climates, selected garden centers, online herb suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris (German cultivar)
varieties.origin: Germany. Selected for cold hardiness.
varieties.traits: Very small evergreen leaves, rounded rather than pointed. Thin stems without reddish coloration. Compact upright growth 15-20cm. Cold-hardy.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Slower spring growth but reliable.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries, online suppliers, selected humid subtropical climates garden centers.

varieties.name: Thymus x citriodorus
varieties.origin: Mediterranean. Natural hybrid of T. vulgaris and T. pulegioides.
varieties.traits: Small shrub 15-30cm tall, spreading 30-45cm. Oval dark green leaves. Pink to lavender flowers in mid-summer. Distinct citrus scent.
varieties.yield_notes: Good to excellent. Vigorous once established.

varieties.name: Thymus serpyllum / Thymus praecox
varieties.origin: Europe, North Africa. Ancient heirloom variety cultivated for thousands of years.
varieties.traits: Very low-growing (2-8cm), mat-forming groundcover. Tiny oval leaves, dense growth. Profuse magenta-pink to purple flowers in summer. Spreads 30-60cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate for culinary use, excellent as ornamental groundcover.
varieties.availability: Available at humid subtropical climates garden centers including specialist nurseries. The Seed Collection Australia offers seeds.

varieties.name: Thymus x citriodorus 'Lime'
varieties.origin: Cultivar selection from lemon thyme group.
varieties.traits: Chartreuse to light green foliage, 15-25cm tall. Compact growth. Pale pink flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. Regular producer.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries, selected humid subtropical climates garden centers.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris 'Orange Balsam'
varieties.origin: Selected cultivar from common thyme.
varieties.traits: Neat, small upright shrub 10-30cm. Narrow orange-scented leaves, gray-green. Lavender flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Compact growth limits harvest volume.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb suppliers, online nurseries.

varieties.name: Thymus herba-barona
varieties.origin: Sardinia and Corsica. Traditionally used to flavor 'Baron of Beef' roasts.
varieties.traits: Low-growing 10-15cm, spreading mat. Narrow dark green leaves. Small pinkish-purple flowers. Strong caraway scent from carvone compound.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Dense growth provides good coverage.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries, online suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus 'Silver Queen' (Lemon Thyme)
varieties.origin: Selected variegated cultivar. Award of Garden Merit winner (Royal Horticultural Society).
varieties.traits: Bushy dwarf 22-30cm tall, spreading 37-45cm. Small ovate dark green leaves edged in silver. Pale mauve flowers. Ornamental value.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. Attractive garnish option.

varieties.name: Thymus citriodorus 'Doone Valley'
varieties.origin: Selected variegated lemon thyme cultivar.
varieties.traits: Mat-forming, 5-10cm tall, spreading 30-60cm. Dark green leaves heavily marked with gold, developing copper-red tones in cold. Lavender-pink flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Ornamental value high.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online herb suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus pseudolanuginosus
varieties.origin: Europe. Ornamental selection.
varieties.traits: Very low-growing 2-5cm, spreading mat to 60cm. Gray-green leaves covered in fine white hairs giving fuzzy texture. Pale pink flowers (sparse).
varieties.yield_notes: Low for culinary purposes. Excellent groundcover.
varieties.availability: Selected humid subtropical climates garden centers, specialist groundcover nurseries.

varieties.name: Thymus serpyllum 'Pink Chintz'
varieties.origin: Selected ornamental creeping thyme.
varieties.traits: Mat-forming 2-5cm tall, spreading 45cm. Fuzzy olive-green to dark green foliage. Salmon-pink to magenta flowers in early spring. First thyme to flower.
varieties.yield_notes: Low culinary. Excellent ornamental.
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates garden centers, groundcover specialists.

varieties.name: Thymus serpyllum 'Elfin' / Thymus praecox 'Elfin'
varieties.origin: Selected miniature cultivar.
varieties.traits: Tiniest thyme variety, 1-3cm tall, spreading 10-20cm slowly. Minute glossy green leaves. Sparse lavender-pink flowers. Moss-like appearance.
varieties.yield_notes: Minimal. Ornamental fairy gardens, rock gardens.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online miniature plant suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus pulegioides cv. Pennsylvania Dutch
varieties.origin: Pennsylvania Dutch heritage variety, USA. Traditional tea herb.
varieties.traits: Larger oval dark green leaves than most thymes. Upright growth to 30cm. Strong scent. Semi-shrub form.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent for tea production. Larger leaves easier to harvest.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris 'Silver Posie'
varieties.origin: Selected variegated common thyme.
varieties.traits: Bushy dwarf 22-30cm, spreading 37-45cm. White-margined gray-green aromatic leaves with pink tips. Bright purple to white flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Good for culinary use and garnish.
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Thymus argenteus 'Hi-Ho Silver'
varieties.origin: Selected variegated cultivar.
varieties.traits: Compact 15-30cm. Green leaves with irregular bright white variegation throughout (more pronounced than Silver Thyme). Pink flowers mid-summer.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Attractive garnish.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online herb suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris 'Argenteus' / Thymus x citriodorus 'Argenteus'
varieties.origin: Selected variegated cultivar with lemon thyme parentage.
varieties.traits: Glossy ivory and gray-green foliage, narrow leaves. Slow-growing, upright 15-25cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate due to slower growth.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus serpyllum 'Annie Hall'
varieties.origin: Selected creeping thyme cultivar.
varieties.traits: Prostrate mat 2-5cm tall, spreading 30-45cm. Tiny narrow glossy green leaves. Pale purple-pink flowers late spring. Excellent bloomer.
varieties.yield_notes: Low culinary, high ornamental.
varieties.availability: Specialty groundcover nurseries.

varieties.name: Thymus pulegioides 'Foxley'
varieties.origin: Selected for exceptional variegation.
varieties.traits: Larger rounded foliage than most thymes. Deep green leaves with cream variegation tinged pink. Highly ornamental. Upright 15-25cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Ornamental value high.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, rare plant suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris
varieties.origin: Mediterranean. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Upright growth 20-30cm. Gray-green leaves, pale flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate in tropical conditions with proper management.
varieties.availability: Limited availability in tropical monsoon climates. Check Ayush tropical monsoon climates (Thrissur online nursery), major tropical monsoon climates garden centers, The Seed Store India online, import seeds from international suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris (French cultivar)
varieties.origin: France/Mediterranean. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Narrower leaves than English thyme. Compact 15-25cm. Reddish stems.
varieties.yield_notes: Low in tropical monsoon climates tropical conditions. High-maintenance.
varieties.availability: Rare in tropical monsoon climates. Special order from specialized nurseries or international seed companies.

varieties.name: Thymus x citriodorus
varieties.origin: Mediterranean hybrid. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Small shrub 15-30cm. Oval dark green leaves. Pink/lavender flowers. Citrus scent.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate with intensive care in tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Thymus vulgaris (German cultivar)
varieties.origin: Germany. Selected for hardiness. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Small evergreen leaves, rounded. Compact 15-20cm. Robust.
varieties.yield_notes: BEST YIELD among thymes for tropical monsoon climates conditions.

varieties.name: Thymus serpyllum / T. praecox
varieties.origin: Europe. Ancient heirloom. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Very low mat-forming groundcover 2-8cm. Tiny leaves. Profuse flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Very poor in tropical monsoon climates monsoon.
varieties.availability: Very limited in tropical monsoon climates. Not recommended to pursue.

varieties.name: Thymus pseudolanuginosus
varieties.origin: Europe. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Very low 2-5cm. Fuzzy gray-green hairy leaves.
varieties.yield_notes: None - will not survive tropical monsoon climates monsoon.
varieties.availability: Not available and not suitable for tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Thymus x citriodorus 'Lime' / T. vulgaris 'Orange Balsam'
varieties.origin: Cultivar selections. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Citrus-scented varieties. Compact growth.
varieties.yield_notes: Poor to moderate in tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: Rare in tropical monsoon climates. Special order from specialty suppliers or international sources.

varieties.name: Thymus herba-barona
varieties.origin: Sardinia/Corsica. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Low-growing 10-15cm. Narrow dark green leaves. Caraway scent.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate potential in tropical monsoon climates with care.
varieties.availability: Very limited in tropical monsoon climates. International seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Thymus pulegioides cv. Pennsylvania Dutch
varieties.origin: Pennsylvania Dutch heritage, USA. Not indigenous to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Larger oval leaves. Upright 30cm. Strong scent.
varieties.yield_notes: Good potential for tropical monsoon climates - larger leaves.

varieties.name: Trachyspermum ammi 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Eastern Mediterranean originally, but naturalized in India for centuries. Considered traditional Indian herb.
varieties.traits: Hardy aromatic annual/perennial herb. Slender stems with feathery, ridged leaves. Small white/pinkish flowers. Height 30-90cm. Belongs to Apiaceae (carrot family), NOT Lamiaceae (mint family) like true thyme.
varieties.yield_notes: EXCELLENT in tropical monsoon climates conditions. Thrives naturally.
varieties.availability: WIDELY AVAILABLE throughout tropical monsoon climates. Ayush tropical monsoon climates (Thrissur), tropical monsoon climates medicinal plant nurseries, local agricultural nurseries, Nursery Nisarga online, tropical monsoon climates Spices Shop, major garden centers, local farmers often have seeds/plants. Malayalam name: Omam (ഓമം).
